title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Production Defense
summary: A release analyzes arbitrary paths without limits, logs text excerpts, follows swapped symlinks, treats a digest as authenticity, cannot reproduce its wheel, and has no rollback or incident drill. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production defense increment in production Bookbot release and residual-risk…
objectives: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.
Gate immutable versioned artifacts on tests, accessibility, security, performance, clean installation, provenance, staged rollout, abort, and rollback evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Production Defense · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Bookbot team handles case 6772 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Defense contract for the supplied data.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_security_recovery_defense_2(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Build Production Defense evidence · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-4eyd94" that uses case 2098 to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-4eyd94
# Competency: bookbot-production-defense.
# Case 2098: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.

def evidence_bookbot_production_defense_4eyd94(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "corpus4eyd94",
        "policy49dye4",
        "revision2098",
        "command4eyd942098",
        "report49dye42098",
        "test20984eyd94",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_4eyd94)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

For repair the production defense defect, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Production Defense, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Bookbot team handles case 4936 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Production Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 4936 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Passing the final happy-path demo makes Bookbot production ready.
